title: The Use of Robotics in Military Surgery: A Scoping Review Protocol
author: John Whitaker

steps.title: Primary Objective
steps.instruction: Primary Objective: To map and characterize the extent, range, and nature of published literature on the use of robotics in military surgical care.

steps.title: To categorise types of robotic technologies used in military surgery
steps.instruction: To categorise types of robotic technologies used in military surgery.

steps.title: To identify research gaps and future directions
steps.instruction: To identify research gaps and future directions.

steps.title: What robotic systems have been described in military surgical contexts
steps.instruction: What robotic systems have been described in military surgical contexts?

steps.title: In what operational environments have robotic...
steps.instruction: In what operational environments have robotic systems been proposed or implemented?

steps.title: What types of surgical procedures are reported
steps.instruction: What types of surgical procedures are reported?

steps.title: What outcomes (clinical, logistical, operational) are described
steps.instruction: What outcomes (clinical, logistical, operational) are described?

steps.title: What barriers and facilitators to implementation are identified
steps.instruction: What barriers and facilitators to implementation are identified?

steps.title: This scoping review will follow the...
steps.instruction: This scoping review will follow the methodological framework developed by Arksey and O'Malley and refined by Levac. Reporting will adhere to the PRISMA-ScR checklist.

steps.title: AI-integrated robotic surgery in military contexts
steps.instruction: AI-integrated robotic surgery in military contexts

steps.title: Primary research studies (quantitative, qualitative, mixed methods)
steps.instruction: Primary research studies (quantitative, qualitative, mixed methods)

steps.title: Military medical doctrine or policy reports
steps.instruction: Military medical doctrine or policy reports

steps.title: Civilian-only robotic surgery with no military application
steps.instruction: Civilian-only robotic surgery with no military application

steps.title: Firm base or planned surgical contexts...
steps.instruction: Firm base or planned surgical contexts with no direct operational or deployed context relevance.

steps.title: The following databases will be searched:
steps.instruction: The following databases will be searched:

steps.title: Pubmed Medline on EBSCO platform CINAHL...
steps.instruction: Pubmed Medline on EBSCO platform CINAHL on EBSCO platform EMBASE on Ovid platform

steps.title: Reference lists of included studies will...
steps.instruction: Reference lists of included studies will be screened for additional sources.

steps.title: A comprehensive search strategy has been...
steps.instruction: A comprehensive search strategy has been developed with a medical librarian to search:

steps.title: Search strategies will be adapted for each database
steps.instruction: Search strategies will be adapted for each database. No date restrictions will initially be applied. There is no language restriction and non-English language publications will be translated using google translate where possible.

steps.title: All identified citations will be imported...
steps.instruction: All identified citations will be imported into a reference management software and duplicates removed.

steps.title: Screening will occur in two stages:
steps.instruction: Screening will occur in two stages:

steps.title: Two independent reviewers will screen studies at each stage
steps.instruction: Two independent reviewers will screen studies at each stage. Discrepancies will be resolved through discussion.

steps.title: A PRISMA flow diagram will document the selection process
steps.instruction: A PRISMA flow diagram will document the selection process.

steps.title: A standardized data extraction form will be developed and pilot-tested
steps.instruction: A standardized data extraction form will be developed and pilot-tested. Extracted data will include:

steps.title: A descriptive analytical approach will be used
steps.instruction: A descriptive analytical approach will be used.

steps.title: Numerical summaries (e
steps.instruction: Numerical summaries (e.g., frequency of study types, countries)

steps.title: Thematic analysis (e
steps.instruction: Thematic analysis (e.g., operational feasibility, ethical concerns, technical challenges)

steps.title: Conceptual mapping of technology maturity levels
steps.instruction: Conceptual mapping of technology maturity levels

steps.title: No formal risk-of-bias assessment will be...
steps.instruction: No formal risk-of-bias assessment will be conducted, consistent with scoping review methodology.

steps.title: Ethical approval is not required as...
steps.instruction: Ethical approval is not required as this review uses publicly available literature.

steps.instruction: Conference presentation in military or surgical forums

steps.instruction: Provide a comprehensive mapping of robotics in military surgery

steps.instruction: Identify maturity gaps between civilian and military applications

steps.instruction: Highlight technological, logistical, and ethical challenges
